data.body: Validation note (decomposition review): the 'push.py already handles these conflicts today, so this only formalises it' framing (inherited from the epic) is slightly understated. push.py:148-175 handles .bridge_state/* only as a DIRTY-WORKING-TREE case (stash->merge->pop); a genuine COMMITTED-content conflict on bindings.json falls through to push.py:177-182 ('real content conflict -> merge --abort'). The tickets branch currently has NO .gitattributes. So WU-3 is net-new, non-redundant work that the WU-2 union merge genuinely needs to converge — not a pure formalisation.
